scope: Authored scenario corpus: measures channel discrimination on known implementation patterns and known false-positive traps (module-homonym bases, same-name-different-domain methods, substring decorator matches). Go scenarios exercise the duck channel exclusively — Go's implicit interfaces have no declared inheritance for the AST channel to walk, and Go has no decorators. It does NOT estimate in-the-wild base rates; n per channel is small and disclosed.

targets.description: Class target: declared subclasses, incl. a module-homonym trap.

labels.why: declared subclass implementing the full protocol

labels.name: CsvPaymentReport
labels.why: subclasses the unrelated reporting.PaymentProvider homonym — name-collision trap

targets.description: Go method target: implicit-interface conformers (duck is Go's native semantics) plus physical/advocacy traps. No AST channel — Go has no declared inheritance.
